2026-04-20T08:12:50.7337443Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T08:12:50.7338000Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T08:12:50.7338445Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T08:12:50.7338809Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T08:12:50.7339125Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T08:12:50.7339368Z [36;1m[0m

If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.","enum":["ASC","DESC"],"type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Filter by labels","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"orderBy":{"description":"Order issues by field. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"query":{"description":"Search query using GitHub issues search syntax","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"sort":{"description":"Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match","enum":["comments","reactions","reactions-+1","reactions--1","reactions-smile","reactions-thinking_face","reactions-heart","reactions-tada","interactions","created","updated"],"type":"string"}},"required":["query"],"type":"object"},"name":"search_issues"}]}}}
2026-04-20T08:15:46.0955329Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T08:12:53.674163446Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"safeoutputs","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"tools":[{"description":"Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). 2026-04-20T08:15:46.0996543Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T08:13:55.155201775Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":"58368","method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-20T08:15:46.1030378Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T08:13:55.401435695Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58368,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document eager pre-handler validation and ResourceNotFoundException.Code/Param\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58341 / commit 16a9c8b7 by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58341 (merged in 1.0.0-beta.3) introduced two significant behavioral changes:\\n\\n1. **Eager pre-handler validation** — `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` are now validated against the provider *before* the handler is invoked. Invalid `previous_response_id` returns HTTP 404; invalid `conversation.id` returns HTTP 400. This happens transparently, before `CreateAsync` is called.\\n\\n2. **`ResourceNotFoundException` gains `Code` and `Param` properties** — mirroring `BadRequestException`, providers can now surface a machine-readable error code and the offending parameter name on 404 responses. The error body is propagated in full to the caller.\\n\\n3. **Full Foundry storage error preservation** — `StorageErrorMapper` now extracts `code`, `message`, `param`, and `type` from upstream storage errors and propagates them; non-400/404/409 upstream status codes are normalized to HTTP 500.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **README Troubleshooting section** does not document that `previous_response_id` (404) and `conversation.id` (400) are validated eagerly before the handler runs, meaning these errors can surface even before `CreateAsync` is invoked.\\n- **Handler implementation guide error table** (`docs/handler-implementation-guide.md`, \\u0026#34;Handler Exceptions\\u0026#34; section) lists `ResourceNotFoundException` with `error.code = null`, but `Code` and `Param` are now non-null public properties — the table needs to reflect that `error.code` is now sourced from the exception, not always null.\\n- **Handler implementation guide `GetHistoryAsync()` section** does not mention that `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` validation is pre-cached: when a handler calls `GetHistoryAsync()`, the resolved item IDs are reused from the pre-handler validation — no extra storage call is made.\\n- **README Key concepts → `ResponseContext` section** does not mention the eager validation guarantee, leaving callers unaware that history fetch errors are surfaced at the framework level before the handler body executes.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — Edit\\n\\nIn the **Troubleshooting → Common errors** section, add two new bullet entries describing the pre-handler validation errors. Insert them after the existing `404 Not Found` bullet:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **404 Not Found** (`previous_response_id`): The `previous_response_id` supplied in the request does not exist in the provider. This error is returned by the framework *before* the handler is invoked — `CreateAsync` is never called.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (`conversation.id`): The `conversation.id` supplied in the request is invalid or rejected by the provider. Like `previous_response_id`, this is validated before the handler runs.\\n```\\n\\nIn the **Key concepts → ResponseContext** section, update the `GetHistoryAsync` bullet to mention the pre-fetch cache:\\n\\nChange:\\n```markdown\\n- **`GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken)`** — returns output items from previous responses in the conversation chain (oldest-first). Uses `previous_response_id` to walk the conversation and resolves items via the provider. Limit controlled by `ResponsesServerOptions.DefaultFetchHistoryCount` (default: 100).\\n```\\nTo:\\n```markdown\\n- **`GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken)`** — returns output items from previous responses in the conversation chain (oldest-first). Uses `previous_response_id` to walk the conversation and resolves items via the provider. Limit controlled by `ResponsesServerOptions.DefaultFetchHistoryCount` (default: 100). The framework validates `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` *before* invoking the handler — if either is invalid the request is rejected with 404/400 immediately. When the handler later calls `GetHistoryAsync()`, the resolved IDs are reused from the pre-fetch cache with no extra storage call.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` — Edit\\n\\nIn the **Error Handling → Handler Exceptions** section, update the `ResourceNotFoundException` row in the error table. Change:\\n\\n```markdown\\n| `ResourceNotFoundException` | 404 | `failed` | `null` | from exception |\\n```\\nTo:\\n```markdown\\n| `ResourceNotFoundException` | 404 | `failed` | from exception (`Code` property, or `null`) | from exception |\\n```\\n\\nAfter the table, add a note:\\n\\n```markdown\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** `ResourceNotFoundException` now exposes `Code` (a machine-readable error code string) and `Param` (the offending parameter name), both nullable. Providers that set these properties will have the values forwarded in the HTTP 404 error envelope. If not set, behavior is unchanged.\\n```\\n\\nIn the **`GetHistoryAsync()`** section, update the bullet list to add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **Pre-validated** — `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` are resolved by the framework before `CreateAsync` is called. If they are invalid, the request fails with 404/400 before the handler runs. When the handler calls `GetHistoryAsync()`, the resolved item IDs are reused from the pre-fetched cache — no additional storage round-trip occurs.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new code snippets are required for these changes — the documentation updates are prose explanations of framework behavior, not new API surface that needs example code.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all required sections:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\nNo sections are missing; only targeted prose edits to existing sections are needed.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm no README placeholder changes are emitted for the prose-only edits\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes expected; verify the output is unchanged\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24655437953/agentic_workflow) · ● 641.8K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58368\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-20T08:11:15Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-20T08:11:15Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

Invalid `previous_response_id` returns HTTP 404; invalid `conversation.id` returns HTTP 400. Insert them after the existing `404 Not Found` bullet:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **404 Not Found** (`previous_response_id`): The `previous_response_id` supplied in the request does not exist in the provider. This error is returned by the framework *before* the handler is invoked — `CreateAsync` is never called.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (`conversation.id`): The `conversation.id` supplied in the request is invalid or rejected by the provider. Like `previous_response_id`, this is validated before the handler runs.\\n```\\n\\nIn the **Key concepts → ResponseContext** section, update the `GetHistoryAsync` bullet to mention the pre-fetch cache:\\n\\nChange:\\n```markdown\\n- **`GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken)`** — returns output items from previous responses in the conversation chain (oldest-first). Uses `previous_response_id` to walk the conversation and resolves items via the provider. Limit controlled by `ResponsesServerOptions.DefaultFetchHistoryCount` (default: 100).\\n```\\nTo:\\n```markdown\\n- **`GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken)`** — returns output items from previous responses in the conversation chain (oldest-first). Uses `previous_response_id` to walk the conversation and resolves items via the provider. Limit controlled by `ResponsesServerOptions.DefaultFetchHistoryCount` (default: 100). The framework validates `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` *before* invoking the handler — if either is invalid the request is rejected with 404/400 immediately. When the handler later calls `GetHistoryAsync()`, the resolved IDs are reused from the pre-fetch cache with no extra storage call.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` — Edit\\n\\nIn the **Error Handling → Handler Exceptions** section, update the `ResourceNotFoundException` row in the error table. Change:\\n\\n```markdown\\n| `ResourceNotFoundException` | 404 | `failed` | `null` | from exception |\\n```\\nTo:\\n```markdown\\n| `ResourceNotFoundException` | 404 | `failed` | from exception (`Code` property, or `null`) | from exception |\\n```\\n\\nAfter the table, add a note:\\n\\n```markdown\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** `ResourceNotFoundException` now exposes `Code` (a machine-readable error code string) and `Param` (the offending parameter name), both nullable. Providers that set these properties will have the values forwarded in the HTTP 404 error envelope. If not set, behavior is unchanged.\\n```\\n\\nIn the **`GetHistoryAsync()`** section, update the bullet list to add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **Pre-validated** — `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` are resolved by the framework before `CreateAsync` is called. If they are invalid, the request fails with 404/400 before the handler runs. When the handler calls `GetHistoryAsync()`, the resolved item IDs are reused from the pre-fetched cache — no additional storage round-trip occurs.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new code snippets are required for these changes — the documentation updates are prose explanations of framework behavior, not new API surface that needs example code.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all required sections:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\nNo sections are missing; only targeted prose edits to existing sections are needed.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm no README placeholder changes are emitted for the prose-only edits\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes expected; verify the output is unchanged\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-20T08:15:46.1197678Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T08:14:18.087920885Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":909,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4090517017,\"number\":57183,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization\",\"body\":\"## Bug description\\nWhen using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.\\n\\nThe exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:\\n`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`\\n\\n## Package and environment\\n- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`\\n- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`\\n- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents\\n- OS: Windows\\n- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`\\n\\n## Expected behavior\\nAfter the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.\\n\\n## Actual behavior\\nThe full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.\\n\\n## Reproduction notes\\n1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.\\n2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.\\n3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.\\n4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.\\n\\n## Minimal observed output\\n```text\\nassistant(stream)\\u003e \\u003cfull answer omitted\\u003e\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent\\nUnhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel\\u003cAzure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall\\u003e.Write(...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()\\n```\\n\\n## Suspected root cause\\nThe generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.\\n\\nRelevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`\\n\\n## Impact\\n- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.\\n- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.\\n- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.\\n\\n## Workaround currently used\\nApplication-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.\\n\\n## Request\\nPlease confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute generation.\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"brandonh-msft\",\"id\":20270743,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjcwNzQz\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20270743?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/brandonh-msft\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1069434707,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Service%20Attention\",\"name\":\"Service Clarify relation to Microsoft.Extensions.Azure\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.Core 1.51.0\\n\\n### Query/Question\\n\\nHi,\\n\\nCould you please clarify how the new [configuration and dependency injection support](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/55491) relates to [Microsoft.Extensions.Azure](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure)'s support for configuration and dependency injection through its [ClientFactory](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/src/Internal/ClientFactory.cs#L95-L340). Reading through [Azure.Core's documentation](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/System.ClientModel/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md) on the subject,
